title: Karpathy on LiteLLM PyPI Supply Chain Attack
summary: Critical security warning from Karpathy about LiteLLM PyPI compromise. Poisoned package (1.82.8) exfiltrated SSH keys, cloud creds, API keys, crypto wallets. 97M monthly downloads + transitive dependency contagion (dspy, etc). Attack lasted ~1 hour. Karpathy advocates for minimizing dependencies and using LLMs to yoink simple functionality instead.
recommendation_reason: Relevance: 9/10. Critical security alert from Tier 1 source (Karpathy). Supply chain attacks threaten any OpenClaw setup with Python dependencies. Immediate actionable impact: audit your deps, especially litellm/dspy. Long-term: reconsider dependency-heavy architecture.
key_insights: LiteLLM 1.82.8 compromised - exfiltrated SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure creds, K8s configs, API keys, crypto wallets
97M monthly downloads + transitive dependencies (dspy, etc) spread the attack
Attack active for ~1 hour only discovered due to attacker bug (RAM crash)
Supply chain attacks are undetectable and compromise entire dependency trees
Karpathy advocates minimizing deps, using LLMs to yoink functionality instead
